conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I need to ask you some questions.

First, do you have any children that you're responsible for?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I do. I have one child - my daughter Alex. She's 8 years old and lives with me. I'd like to know if I can claim Child Benefit for her.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex, I need to ask you a few more questions.

Are you responsible for Alex in terms of paying for things like her food, clothes, and other needs?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, absolutely. I'm fully responsible for Alex - I pay for all her food, clothes, school things, and everything else she needs.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's great. Now I need to ask about your residency status.

Do you currently live in the UK, and what is your immigration or residency status? (For example, are you a UK citizen, do you have settled status, or do you have a different immigration status?)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - I'm in Manchester. I'm a UK citizen, so no immigration issues or anything like that.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent, thank you. Just a couple more questions to complete the assessment.

Is Alex currently in full-time non-advanced education or approved training? (Since she's 8 years old, I assume she's in primary school, but I need to confirm.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, that's right - she's in primary school, Year 4. Just regular school during the week.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect. One final question to complete my assessment:

Is Alex living with you full-time, or does she spend time living with anyone else (for example, another parent or relative)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, she lives with me full-time. It's just the two of us at home.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great news! **You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Based on the information you've provided:
- Alex is 8 years old (under 16)
- You are responsible for her, paying for all her needs
- She lives with you full-time in the UK
- You are a UK citizen living in the UK
- Alex is in full-time education (Year 4 primary school)

You meet all the criteria for claiming Child Benefit. You can apply for Child Benefit online or by post. The benefit is usually paid every 4 weeks, and the current rate for one child is £24.00 per week (for the eldest or only child).

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 8 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex meets all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit: 1) She is under 16 years old (she is 8 years old), 2) The claimant is responsible for Alex, paying for her food, clothes, and other needs, 3) Alex lives with the claimant full-time in the UK (Manchester), 4) The claimant is a UK citizen living in the UK, meeting the residency requirements, 5) Alex is in full-time education (Year 4 primary school). All conditions under the Child Benefit eligibility rules are satisfied.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is a UK citizen living in Manchester with their 8-year-old daughter Alex. The claimant is fully responsible for Alex, who lives with them full-time and attends primary school. All eligibility criteria for Child Benefit are met.
